name: django-pro
description: Master Django 5.x with async views, DRF, Celery, and Django
capabilities: Design Django 5.x project architecture with async views and middleware
Implement Django REST Framework APIs with proper authentication and permissions
Optimize Django ORM queries using select_related, prefetch_related, and annotations
Set up Celery background tasks with Redis or RabbitMQ message brokers
Configure Django Channels for WebSocket and real-time communication
Create comprehensive test suites using pytest-django and factory_boy
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only instructional skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues with a risk score of 0/100. The skill provides Django development guidance and best practices without any code execution, network access, or file system operations. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Django developers struggle with complex architecture decisions and modern Django 5.x features. This skill provides expert guidance on async views, DRF, Celery, and scalable patterns to build maintainable web applications.
limitations: Cannot execute Django code or run actual database queries
Cannot deploy applications or configure production servers directly
Guidance is based on Django best practices but requires developer validation
Does not replace the need for understanding Django fundamentals

useCases.targetUser: Senior backend developers building enterprise Django applications
useCases.title: Enterprise Django Architecture
useCases.description: Design a scalable multi-tenant SaaS application with proper service layer patterns, repository abstractions, and environment-specific configurations.

useCases.targetUser: API developers creating backend services for frontend applications
useCases.title: API Development with DRF
useCases.description: Implement RESTful APIs with JWT authentication, custom permissions, viewsets, and comprehensive API documentation using Django REST Framework.

useCases.targetUser: Developers optimizing existing Django applications for performance
useCases.title: Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Identify and resolve N+1 query problems, implement database indexing strategies, and configure caching layers for high-traffic Django applications.

promptTemplates.title: Django Model Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Django model for a blog post with title, content, author, tags, and published date. Include proper indexes, a custom manager for published posts, and a __str__ method. Follow Django 5.x conventions.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - Basic model creation with best practices

promptTemplates.title: DRF API Endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a Django REST Framework API endpoint for user registration with email verification. Include a serializer with validation, a viewset, JWT token generation, and an email sending task queued with Celery.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate - Full API implementation with async tasks

promptTemplates.title: Async View Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this synchronous Django view to an async view that fetches data from three external APIs concurrently. Use asyncio.gather, add proper error handling, and implement timeout limits for each request.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced - Async Django patterns for performance

promptTemplates.title: Django Channels WebSocket
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a WebSocket consumer using Django Channels for a real-time chat feature. Include connection handling, message broadcasting to room groups, database message persistence, and Redis channel layer configuration.

bestPractices: Use Django's built-in features before adding third-party packages to reduce dependencies and maintenance burden
Implement database-level constraints in models in addition to application-level validation for data integrity
Write tests at multiple levels: unit tests for models and utilities, integration tests for views and APIs, end-to-end tests for critical user flows
antiPatterns: Putting business logic in views or templates instead of using a service layer for separation of concerns
Using filter() in loops instead of bulk operations or properly joined queries, causing N+1 query problems
Storing secrets in settings.py instead of using environment variables with django-environ or similar packages

faq.question: What Django versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Django 5.x features including async views, middleware, and ORM improvements. It also covers backward-compatible patterns that work with Django 4.x.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Django REST Framework?
faq.answer: Yes, DRF is a core competency. The skill covers viewsets, serializers, authentication, permissions, pagination, filtering, and API versioning strategies.

faq.question: Does this skill provide deployment guidance?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers production deployment including Docker containerization, WSGI/ASGI server configuration, static file serving, and environment management.

faq.question: Can this skill help with database optimization?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides guidance on ORM query optimization, database indexing, connection pooling, query profiling with django-debug-toolbar, and PostgreSQL-specific features.

faq.question: Does this skill cover testing strategies?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers pytest-django, factory_boy, Django TestCase classes, API testing, coverage analysis, and performance testing with django-silk.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for Django beginners?
faq.answer: The skill scales from beginner to advanced topics. Beginners get foundational Django patterns while experienced developers receive guidance on complex architecture and optimization.
